raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "A huge body of research links adverse health impacts and the decline in pollinator populations to the use of pesticides, particularly neonicotinoids.",
    "California beekeepers lost 41.9% of their colonies last year, one of the worst years on record.",
    "The European Union has already banned many neonicotinoid pesticides altogether.",
    "Neonicotinoids are the world’s most widely used insecticides and are incredibly toxic to pollinators.",
    "Just one square foot of grass treated with a typical neonic lawn product can contain enough neonics to kill one million bees.",
    "Most neonicotinoid chemicals stay in the soil for years and can be carried by rain or lawn watering.",
    "Animal studies have connected neonicotinoids to reproductive harms, reduced thyroid function, increased offspring mortality, and other damaging health effects.",
    "Monitoring by the California Department of Pesticide Regulation detected imidacloprid in 92% of water samples taken in Southern California’s urban areas and 58% of Northern California’s urban samples.",
    "Most uses of neonicotinoids in non-agricultural settings are completely unnecessary."
  ]
}
```
